ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for red bean mochi soy milk lava bun. FOODON_03315021 (steamed bun) is the closest defensible parent category. This bun uses a sponge-dough method and may be baked or steamed.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Sweet buns and rolls) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for mochi-filled soy milk lava buns.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific filled sweet bun product.

preparation_method: topped with soybean cream and soybean flour

serving.natural_language_portion: One bun weighing approximately 100 g, served as a single-portion dessert or snack item.

nutrient_source.title: Sweet red bean bun (anpan-style) and mochi-filled bun composite estimate; reference anchored to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Sweet roll (plain) and red bean paste (anko)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented by FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for mochi and soybean flour components
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific product. Values are estimated by component weighting: sweet enriched dough (~45%), red bean paste (~25%), mochi (~15%), soybean cream and soybean flour topping (~15%). USDA SR Legacy entries used: Sweet roll plain (FDC ID 18350), Red bean paste (anko, FDC ID 168591), Mochi (FDC ID 168880), Soy flour full-fat (FDC ID 16117). Weighted composite per 100 g calculated. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweet buns and red bean paste products; analogous estimate based on 'Steamed bun (mantou)' GI=68 and 'Red bean paste (anko)' GI=43 from University of Sydney GI Database; weighted composite estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for red bean mochi soy milk lava bun. GI estimated as weighted composite: sweet enriched dough component (GI ~65, analogous to enriched sweet roll), red bean paste component (GI ~43, University of Sydney GI Database entry for azuki bean paste), mochi component (GI ~61, University of Sydney GI Database entry for mochi rice cake), soybean flour topping (GI ~25, low-GI soy component). Weighted average across components by carbohydrate contribution yields estimated GI ~55 (low). Glycemic load per 100 g serving = (55 × 50.5) / 100 = 27.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~185 mg per 100 g) and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to one bun per occasion and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods, as the sodium content from enriched dough and soybean cream contributes meaningfully to daily sodium targets of under 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate content (~50.5 g per 100 g) with low glycemic index (~55) and high sugar content (~18 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this bun as an occasional indulgence only, limiting to half a bun per serving and pairing with a protein or fibre source to blunt the postprandial glucose response, given the low GI and high sugar load from red bean paste and soybean cream. While the GI is technically 'low', the overall carbohydrate and sugar load is significant.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.1 g per 100 g), cholesterol (~28 mg per 100 g), and refined sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should consume this bun sparingly, as the combination of saturated fat from butter or cream components and added sugars from red bean paste and soybean cream contributes to atherogenic dietary patterns when consumed frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Red Bean Mochi and Soy Milk Lava Bun is a Chinese-Japanese fusion sweet bun sold in Singapore, originating from specialty dessert bakeries such as RUXU (入续 糖水铺与包). The bun is made using the sponge dough method, which produces a particularly soft, pillowy, and rich crumb texture. The filling consists of homemade low-sugar sweet red bean paste (azuki bean paste) combined with chestnut pieces and soft, chewy mochi (glutinous rice cake). The exterior is topped with a soybean cream lava layer and generously dusted with soybean flour (kinako), giving the bun a distinctive nutty, earthy aroma and a melt-in-the-mouth quality. The overall flavour profile is mildly sweet, with contrasting textures from the soft dough, chewy mochi, and smooth bean paste. Nutritionally, one bun (~100 g) provides approximately 278 kcal, 50.5 g carbohydrate, 6.2 g protein, and 5.8 g fat, with a low glycemic index of approximately 55. The red bean filling contributes dietary fibre (~2.8 g per 100 g) and plant-based iron. The soybean components add isoflavones and modest protein. Clinically, this bun is a moderate-to-high concern food for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI but significant overall carbohydrate and sugar content from the red bean paste and soybean cream. It is a moderate concern for hypertension due to sodium from the enriched dough. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a daily staple. The dish reflects the broader Singapore trend of Japanese-influenced Chinese bakery items, combining traditional Chinese tangshui (sweet soup) dessert culture with Japanese mochi and kinako aesthetics.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific product exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by component weighting from USDA SR Legacy entries for sweet roll, red bean paste, mochi, and soy flour. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for closest component foods. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from 'Beverages' (input error) to 'Desserts', as this is a filled sweet bun, not a beverage. 2. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates; no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific product. All component reference entries are from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC IDs: 18350, 168591, 168880, 16117). 3. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero: sweet buns and bean paste products contain negligible vitamin C; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy component data. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1.0 — not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for this estimate; sentinel value applied. 5. selenium_mcg is -1.0 — not consistently reported across the component sources used; sentinel value applied. 6. GI is an analogous estimate (~55, low) based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for steamed bun (mantou), azuki bean paste, mochi, and soy flour; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. Glycemic load per serving (100 g) = (55 × 50.5) / 100 = 27.8. 7. Halal status is assumed based on typical ingredients; individual restaurant certification should be verified. 8. The input food_group 'Beverages' was an error in the seed record and has been corrected to 'Desserts'. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy fat profile of sweet enriched rolls and soy components.
